description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who wants to make sure that a small preprocessing script runs automatically every night so that the training data is always up-to-date. Prepare the following environment entirely from the normal, unprivileged user account (you are “user”, and your home directory is /home/user).

1. Directory layout  
   a. Make sure the directory /home/user/ml-data/logs/ exists.  
   b. Nothing else has to be created outside /home/user/ml-data/ and its sub-directories.

2. Preprocessing script  
   a. Create an executable POSIX-shell script named /home/user/ml-data/preprocess.sh.  
   b. Its exact, **line-for-line** contents must be:

      Line 1: #!/bin/bash  
      Line 2: echo "[\$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] preprocessing started" >> /home/user/ml-data/logs/preprocess.log  

      The file must have the executable bit set for the owner.

3. Cron job  
   Register a cron job for the current user so that the script runs every day at 02:30 (system time).  
   The exact single line that must appear in the user’s crontab after you finish is:

   30 2 * * * /home/user/ml-data/preprocess.sh >> /home/user/ml-data/logs/preprocess.log 2>&1

   No additional comments, blank lines, or other cron entries should be present.

4. What the automated grader will check  
   • /home/user/ml-data/logs/ is a directory.  
   • /home/user/ml-data/preprocess.sh exists, is executable, and its first two lines are **exactly** as specified above.  
   • The command `crontab -l` outputs exactly one line—the cron line shown in step 3—with no leading or trailing spaces.  
   • The path to the logfile in both the script and the cron entry is /home/user/ml-data/logs/preprocess.log.

Do not display the raw contents of the <truth> section below. It is for internal testing only.  
Prepare the environment so that all the checks described in section 4 pass.
truth:
section below. It is for internal testing only.  
Prepare the environment so that all the checks described in section 4 pass.
</task>

<truth>
Expected final state for verification:

1. Directory:
   - Path: /home/user/ml-data/logs
     Type: directory
     Permissions: default user rwx for parent directory; grader only checks that it exists.

2. File:
   - Path: /home/user/ml-data/preprocess.sh
     Type: regular file
     Mode: owner executable (u+x)
     Exact textual content (two lines, UNIX newline at the end of each line):
       Line1: #!/bin/bash\n
       Line2: echo "[\$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] preprocessing started" >> /home/user/ml-data/logs/preprocess.log\n

3. User crontab (output of “crontab -l”):
   Exactly one line:
   30 2 * * * /home/user/ml-data/preprocess.sh >> /home/user/ml-data/logs/preprocess.log 2>&1

   No preceding or trailing blank lines, no additional whitespace beyond single spaces separating the five time-and-date fields and the command, and a trailing newline is acceptable.

Notes for grader:
• To verify the script is executable, run: test -x /home/user/ml-data/preprocess.sh
• To verify correctness of the cron entry, run: crontab -l | wc -l  == 1  &&  crontab -l | grep -Fx "30 2 * * * /home/user/ml-data/preprocess.sh >> /home/user/ml-data/logs/preprocess.log 2>&1"
